name: Camino Viejo del Puerto-Pantano Plasencia – circular desde Plasencia
region_intro: Mountain biking around Plasencia offers diverse natural landscapes, characterized by extensive dehesas of holm and cork oaks, alongside grasslands and riparian vegetation along the Jerte River. The region features varied terrain, from the river's banks to hills reaching up to 998 meters at Pico Gordo, providing a range of elevations for cycling. The Valcorchero dehesa, in particular, presents a rich natural environment for exploration. Best mountain bike trails around Plasencia The most popular mountain bike trail is Camino Viejo del Puerto-Pantano Plasencia – circular desde Plasencia, a 16.8 miles (27.0 km) difficult trail that takes 2 hours 22 minutes to complete, offering a challenging circular route. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Plasencia Upstream River Trail, a moderate 12.1 miles (19.6 km) path. This route follows the Jerte River, providing scenic views along its banks. Local mountain bikers also love the Vía Verde de la Plata – Béjar Pass loop from Plasencia, a 63.6 miles (102.4 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 6 hours 36 minutes. Mountain biking around Plasencia is defined by dehesas of holm and cork oaks, the Jerte River, and a mix of hills and open woodlands. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside paths to more challenging climbs. The routes in Plasencia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 600 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Plasencia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there around Plasencia?
A: There are around 50 mountain bike trails in the Plasencia area, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. These include 6 easy routes, 34 moderate routes, and 9 difficult routes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Plasencia?
A: The terrain around Plasencia is quite diverse. You'll find extensive &lt;em&gt;dehesas&lt;/em&gt; of holm and cork oaks, open grasslands, and riparian vegetation along the Jerte River. The landscape also features hills and mountains, with altitudes reaching up to 998 meters at Pico Gordo, providing varied elevations for your rides.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Plasencia offers 6 easy mountain bike trails. While specific family-friendly routes aren't detailed, easy trails are generally a good starting point. The region's riverside paths can be particularly gentle. Always check the route details for elevation and surface to ensure it meets your family's needs.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Plasencia?
A: Yes, many routes around Plasencia are circular. For example, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009663715/camino-viejo-del-puerto-pantano-plasencia-circular-desde-plasencia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Viejo del Puerto-Pantano Plasencia – circular desde Plasencia&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option. Another great loop is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40009477" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plasencia Reservoir – Isla Park loop from Plasencia&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you around the reservoir.

Q: What are some scenic mountain bike trails with good views?
A: Many trails offer scenic views, especially those along the Jerte River or through the dehesas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021563508/plasencia-upstream-river-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plasencia Upstream River Trail&lt;/a&gt; provides beautiful views along the riverbanks. Routes that climb into the hills, such as those near Pico Gordo, will also offer panoramic vistas of the surrounding natural landscapes.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Plasencia?
A: The mountain bike trails in Plasencia are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.0 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 600 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the area, often praising the varied terrain and the natural beauty of the dehesas and river landscapes.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural attractions I can visit near the mountain bike trails?
A: Absolutely. Plasencia's rich history means there are several attractions. You can explore the historic city center, including the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6517874" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plasencia Cathedral&lt;/a&gt; and the lively &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6517887" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor&lt;/a&gt;. Historic bridges like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5135059" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Lázaro Bridge&lt;/a&gt; also offer picturesque elements along the Jerte River.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by lakes or reservoirs?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate the local reservoirs. The moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40009477" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plasencia Reservoir – Isla Park loop from Plasencia&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, taking you past the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5391554" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Plasencia&lt;/a&gt;. These areas often provide tranquil settings and additional scenic points.

Q: What is the longest mountain bike trail available in Plasencia?
A: One of the longest routes is the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19433717" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de la Plata – Béjar Pass loop from Plasencia&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 102 kilometers. This extensive trail leads through varied landscapes and is typically completed in about 6 hours and 36 minutes, making it suitable for experienced riders looking for a full-day adventure.

Q: Is it possible to reach the mountain bike trails using public transport?
A: While Plasencia is well-connected by road, specific public transport options directly to trailheads can vary. It's advisable to check local bus schedules or consider bringing your bike on regional transport if available. Many routes, however, start directly from or near the city center, making them accessible.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for advanced riders looking for a challenge?
A: Yes, Plasencia offers 9 difficult mountain bike trails for advanced riders. These routes often feature significant elevation gains and technical sections. A notable challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009663715/camino-viejo-del-puerto-pantano-plasencia-circular-desde-plasencia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Viejo del Puerto-Pantano Plasencia – circular desde Plasencia&lt;/a&gt;, which includes over 550 meters of elevation gain.
tips: El Puerto is an interesting historical site, where the trail meets the road and has great views of the valley and reservoir.
A good place to stop and enjoy the view. The Plasencia Reservoir was built in the 80s on the Jerte River.

The Parque de la Isla in Plasencia is one of the main green lungs of the city and a perfect place to enjoy nature. Located in the urban section of the Jerte River, the park is a river island that stretches for almost 4 kilometers.

waypoints.name: Sanctuary of Our Lady of Remedies
